quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of familial stress moved into the board's Leading section, driven by an uncle's resume spamming. The tension was palpable.

A front of separatist sentiment blew into Discourse, as one anon suggested North India separate from South India and call itself Aryavarta. The post drew a scattered response.

Meanwhile, a heat wave of caste-based claims swept through Notices, with one anon asserting upper castes can thrive without meat, while dalits suffer.

Another post extended an invitation to Paki posters, while denigrating Southie posters, sparking a brief but intense downpour of replies. The board's atmosphere remained charged, with a high pressure system of skin tone controversy persisting in Discourse.

Anon questioned the media's representation of dark-skinned individuals, sparking a debate.

articles.title: Uncle's resume spamming causes familial stress
articles.body:
Anon expressed frustration with their uncle, who has been spamming Anon's resume to various people, causing Anon to feel anxious about potential calls.

Anon's uncle has also been advising Anon to be prepared to receive calls at any time, claiming that he has been sharing Anon's resume with others.

One might wonder why Anon's uncle would do such a thing, but no explanation was offered in the post.

Anon mentioned that seeing their uncle's phone number now triggers a mini-heart attack, indicating a significant deterioration in their relationship over the past few months.

The post did not specify what Anon's uncle hoped to achieve by sharing the resume or what kind of calls Anon expected to receive as a result.

The situation seems to have caused considerable distress for Anon, who is evidently uncomfortable with their uncle's actions.

articles.title: Anon Suggests North India Separate, Call Itself Aryavarta
articles.body:
Anon proposed North India separate itself from South India and adopt the name Aryavarta. The poster claimed North Indians share greater cultural, linguistic, and genetic similarities with people from Pakistan, Afghanistan, or Iran than with South Indians.

Another anon responded with hostility, calling the OP a 'bhangi ki aulad' and telling them to 'go do something.' This respondent also questioned the practicality of the proposal, saying 'Why the fuck is it always “we should do this” haan saale aap order doh hum abhi ready karte hai aryavarta sir.' One respondent expressed skepticism about the OP's idea, stating that such proposals are often mere 'larping' and that the poster should focus on 'read[ing] a book, make[ing] friends, or do[ing] something useful in life.' The discussion also involved exchanges about caste and religion.

Anon mentioned 'semitic religions are cancer' and another poster claimed Rajputs are 'muzzie' and allied with 'anti-aryan forces.' The thread included various personal attacks and insults.

For example, one poster told another they 'cry about oppression depression in rajasthan.' The topic of British influence on Indian unity was brought up, with one poster stating that India was unified only to serve British interests.

Anti-Semitic remarks appeared in the discussion. Anon accused 'Jewz' of controlling Islam and Christianity, while another poster suggested the OP might be using an Israeli VPN or be a 'real Jew.'

articles.title: Skin tone representation in media
articles.body:
Anon questioned why media and pornography often depict pajeetas as dark-skinned, claiming that half of them are fair-skinned in reality.

Another anon responded with a dermatological study, stating that roughly 74% to 76% of Indian women have "brown" to "dark brown" complexions, while only about 8% to 10% are fair-skinned.

A respondent disputed the original poster's claim, saying average North Indian skin tone is similar to Bollywood actors.

They argued that the Indo-Gangetic plain, where many North Indians live, justifies fair-skinned individuals not relating to the "timmy stereotype" of dark-skinned individuals.

Anon also claimed that the majority of the subcontinent's population has medium brown skin and that the Indo-Gangetic plain is more populated than South India, with a large population of fair-skinned people.

One respondent joked that the original poster would be called derogatory names like "Lungiland dweller" and would claim everyone around them is "milk white." The discussion continued with claims about skin tone distribution in India, including a statement that upper-caste North Indians are white, while lower-caste and South Indians are black.

Some anons disputed these claims, with one stating that Northwest low castes are even fairer than Gangetic upper castes.

articles.title: Paki posters invited, Southie posters denigrated
articles.body:
Anon asked if Pakistanis are banned on Indiachan and offered to invite Pakistani friends. Another anon welcomed the idea, saying it would be better than current Southie posters.

One respondent called Pakistanis 'high iq, fair skinned aryan brothers' and preferable to 'dalits.' A different anon expressed frustration with current Southie posters, who they claimed kept spamming threads about certain topics.

Several users hurled insults at each other, including casteist and nationalist slurs.

articles.title: Anon Claims Upper Castes Can Thrive Without Meat
articles.body:
Anon posted that Upper Castes (UCs) can do well without meat, attributing this to their genetic components and ancestral dietary practices.

They claimed UCs are mainly Zagros, Steppe, and AASI, and that farmers and cattle herders can survive without meat.

Another anon countered that vegetarianism is a relatively new phenomenon outside Northwest India, adopted by some lower-caste groups as part of Sanskritization to shed social stigma.

One respondent also noted that many brahmins, particularly from middle-upper classes or urban populations, consume meat. Anon also made derogatory remarks about dalits and Muslims, while another user asked for satiating veg recipes.

One respondent stated brahmins don't eat meat except for KP's.
